zone_6b_notes: One of the most reliable, indestructible perennials in Zone 6b â€” thrives on neglect in Farmington. Stella de Oro (gold, reblooming) and Happy Returns (yellow, extended season) are far superior to the common orange ditch lily for garden use. Modern reblooming hybrids bloom May through frost with proper deadheading. Divide clumps every 4â€“5 years when flowering declines. Drought tolerant once established. Deer will eat them; if deer pressure is high, plant reblooming cultivars where deer rarely travel or protect with fencing. Each flower lasts one day but clumps carry dozens of buds.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant bare-root fans or divisions spring or fall
